title: Rest, consolidation, and preview for Google Course 5: Assets, threats, and vulnerabilities (part 1)
week_focus: Google Course 5: Assets, threats, and vulnerabilities (part 1)
checkpoint: Can classify assets and explain why not all data deserves the same treatment.
resource_names: Google Course 5: Assets, Threats, and Vulnerabilities
plain_language_intro: Today is a light consolidation day for google course 5: assets, threats, and vulnerabilities (part 1).
mini_lecture_markdown:
Today is a rest day for Google Course 5: Assets, threats, and vulnerabilities (part 1).

Keep the week lightly in view, but do not turn the day into a full study session. Next, the roadmap moves into google course 5: assets, threats, and vulnerabilities (part 2).

The main goal is to preserve continuity without adding another heavy block of material.

A short preview of the next week is enough to keep continuity without turning the day back into a long session.
exam_relevance_markdown: Rest days matter indirectly for Google Cybersecurity Professional Certificate because spaced retrieval and reduced overload help you keep earlier concepts available when scenario questions combine multiple topics.
key_distinctions: Recognition vs diagnosis — knowing the term name is not enough; know what evidence would make you pick it in a scenario.
mental_model_markdown: Picture the week as a simple chain of concept, mechanism, evidence, and decision.
self_check_prompts: Walk through today's objective in sequence and say which step would fail first if the system were misconfigured.
Connect today's glossary terms to this week's deliverable: what part of the artifact proves that you understand the mechanism and not just the label?
What idea from this week would still confuse you if it appeared inside a short scenario tomorrow?
quick_recap: Today keeps google course 5: assets, threats, and vulnerabilities (part 1) organized without adding more heavy content.
